title: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum
what_description: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with a height range of 30-60 cm, featuring a taproot and a branched stem with linear, lanceolate to oval-shaped leaves that are 2-5 cm long and 0.5-2 cm wide. The plant produces solitary, terminal flower heads with 10-20 florets, each measuring 1-2 cm in diameter, and having pink to red coloration. The flowers are hermaphroditic, with five-merous corollas and a white to yellowish center. Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum is native to Central Asia.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are inflammation, fever, and rheumatism, which are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling. Its antipyretic properties also help to combat fever by lowering body temperature, while its antirheumatic properties alleviate joint pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism. Additionally, Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum has been used to treat digestive issues, such as gastritis and ulcers, by reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes. Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating insect bites, skin irritation, and sore throats, as well as relieving muscle pain and headaches by reducing inflammation and pain. Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum is also used to treat infections, such as gastroenteritis, and to alleviate symptoms of eczema, arthritis, menstrual cramps, wounds, and cold sores, as well as fungal infections, by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are primarily due to its antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory activity of this plant is a result of the saponins and terpenoids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating pain. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are owed to the volatile oils, such as thujone and borneol, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, making it effective against a range of pathogens. The plant's analgesic properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which inhibit the production of prostaglandins and reduce pain perception. Furthermore, the antispasmodic properties of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are due to the flavonoids and terpenoids, which relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, making it effective in treating conditions such as asthma and bronchitis.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are tanacetin, α-pinene, camphor, β-pinene, and caryophyllene, which have been identified as the primary bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Tanacetin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while α-pinene and β-pinene have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Camphor, a terpene, has been demonstrated to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, and caryophyllene has been identified as a potent anti-inflammatory agent with potential for cancer prevention. The presence of these constituents in Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum is attributed to its chemical composition, specifically the combination of sesquiterpene lactones and terpenes, which are biosynthesized through the mevalonate pathway in the plant's tissues. The specific concentrations and ratios of these constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's growing conditions, soil quality, and stage of development.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are tincture, infusion, tea, salve, and essential oil. The tincture is a concentrated liquid solution of the plant's bioactive compounds, obtained through solvent extraction, typically used for medicinal purposes. The infusion is a liquid extract made by steeping the dried flowers in hot water, often used for its relaxing and anti-inflammatory effects. The salve is a topical preparation, typically made from the infused oil, used to treat skin irritations and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil, obtained through steam distillation, is used for its aromatic properties and has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and as a natural insect repellent.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum are experience nausea, develop skin irritation, induce nausea, interact with medications, and feel dizziness. These side effects can occur due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, particularly coccineuside, which can be toxic in high concentrations. Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum contains compounds that can cause gastrointestinal irritation, leading to nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset. Skin irritation may also occur due to allergic reactions or contact dermatitis. Additionally, the herb may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and cause dizziness due to its potential effect on blood pressure and circulation.
